summary: DHH shares his discovery of audio books through the combination of iTunes 3 and Audible.com, praising the seamless digital purchase and download experience. He highlights the practical benefits of listening to books on an iPod during commutes and while working at a desk. The post reflects early enthusiasm for digital media consumption and Apple's ecosystem.
key_points: The iTunes 3 and Audible.com integration makes purchasing and downloading audiobooks remarkably simple
iPod audiobooks solve practical problems: listening on a crowded bus without needing a seat, and multitasking at a desk
Early adoption of digital media consumption patterns that would become mainstream years later
money_quotes: Pick your title, pay the man, and choose your sound quality.
With the iPod, you can "read" books comfortably in the bus without requiring a seat, and at the desktop, you can work and listen at the same time.
key_insight: The combination of digital storefronts and portable devices was already transforming media consumption in 2002, making audiobooks practical for everyday multitasking.
